topic.what: Oldest surviving complete Latin medical text with clinical descriptions of stomach inflammation and dietary management.
topic.how: Systematic clinical observation emphasizing empirical description over humoral theory, with practical dietary recommendations.
topic.why_matters: Celsus's emphasis on observation over theory represents the earliest empiricist approach to gastric disease, anticipating the clinical method by 1800 years.

characteristics.what_it_measures: Quality and specificity of symptom descriptions
characteristics.value: Recognizable gastritis symptoms described with clinical precision
characteristics.vs_baseline: More clinically precise than Hippocratic descriptions

novelty: Earliest clinical (non-theoretical) description of gastric inflammation in Latin literature
Dietary management recommendations remarkably aligned with modern functional approaches
Emphasis on fasting as therapeutic — still debated in modern gastritis management
open_problems: Celsus was likely an encyclopedist, not a practicing physician — limits clinical authority
